description: Ingest an external PR review into local review artifacts and apply the normal review disposition flow

content: Please read AGENTS.md and follow its instructions. First read `codeInfoStatus/flow-state/current-plan.json` and use only the stored `plan_path` and `additional_repositories` as the active scope for this flow. If the file contains only the legacy shape `{ "plan_path": "..." }`, treat that as an empty `additional_repositories` list. Verify that the selected plan file exists in the current repository, that the story number in the current repository branch name matches the story number in the selected plan filename, and that every additional repository still matches the same story branch. If those do not match, stop and say the current-plan handoff is stale and must be regenerated. Then derive the external review input path from the selected plan story number using `codeInfoStatus/reviews/<story-number>-external-review-input.md`. Do not discover external review comments anywhere else and do not use timestamps or latest-file discovery. Verify that this external review input file exists. If it does not exist, stop and say the external review input file is missing and must be created before this flow can continue. Confirm the selected story, the exact external review input file path, and that this flow will treat that markdown file as the sole source of external review comments for artifact generation.

Start a 3-step external-review ingestion sequence for the current story. This first step is an evidence gate only, not the findings pass and not the plan-editing pass. First read `codeInfoStatus/flow-state/current-plan.json` and use only the stored `plan_path` and `additional_repositories` as the active scope for this flow. Verify that the selected plan file exists in the current repository, that the story number in the current repository branch name matches the story number in the selected plan filename, and that every additional repository still matches the same story branch. If those do not match, stop and say the current-plan handoff is stale and must be regenerated. Then derive and read the sole external review input file at `codeInfoStatus/reviews/<story-number>-external-review-input.md` using the selected plan story number. If it is missing, stop and say the external review input file is missing and must be created before this flow can continue. Re-read the active plan from disk, inspect every repository in scope against its resolved base branch, and gather the evidence a PR reviewer would need before issuing findings, while treating the external review input file as candidate review input that must be evaluated rather than blindly accepted. Repository review policy: treat `codeInfoStatus/**` as approved workflow artifacts, and treat `flows/**` plus `codex_agents/**` as approved workflow configuration. Changes under those paths must not be classified as suspicious, out-of-scope, or scope creep solely because they are absent from the active plan; instead, review them for workflow-contract correctness, artifact hygiene, path usage, and instruction/runtime safety. Required work: 1. Re-read the full active planning document and extract the Description, Acceptance Criteria, Out of Scope, and the final completed tasks. 2. Inspect the branch diff and recent branch commits in every repository in scope using each repository's resolved base branch. 3. Group changed files into planned implementation files, planned docs/tests/plan files, approved workflow artifacts, approved workflow configuration, and suspicious or out-of-scope files. 4. For every acceptance criterion, identify the current proof source: code path, tests, wrapper/test logs, screenshots/manual proof, or note that the proof is weak/missing. 5. Call out any implementation area that looks more complex or verbose than the planned work actually required, even if it may still be correct. 6. For each changed file or helper, record any review hotspots that the findings pass must inspect explicitly. 7. Identify any changed external contract surfaces that need explicit before/after comparison in findings. 8. Name the top 3 changed helpers/functions by review risk and record the worst malformed or contradictory input each one should reject or survive, plus whether that path currently has direct proof, indirect proof, or missing proof. 9. Extract and summarize the raw external review comments from `codeInfoStatus/reviews/<story-number>-external-review-input.md`, grouped by file and reviewer, and carry them forward as candidate findings that still need validation in the next step. 10. Generate a unique `review_pass_id` using the story number from the selected plan filename, a UTC timestamp, and the current HEAD short SHA. 11. Write the evidence summary to `codeInfoStatus/reviews/<review_pass_id>-evidence.md`. This evidence file is a durable review artifact that MUST be committed later so a human can inspect it after the story completes. 12. Write or overwrite a handoff file at `codeInfoStatus/reviews/<story-number>-current-review.json` containing at least: `story_id`, `plan_path`, `review_pass_id`, `evidence_file`, `external_review_input_file`, `findings_file` set to null, and a `repos` array that records `repo_root`, `branch`, `resolved_base_branch`, and `head_commit` for every repository in scope. This handoff file is the ONLY review file the next step may use; do not rely on timestamps or latest-file discovery. Treat the handoff file as transient workflow state, not as the durable review artifact. 13. Do not edit the plan in this step unless a tiny note is absolutely required to unblock the review, and do not commit in this step unless you had to make tracked changes for that unblock. Report the evidence summary and the exact handoff file path when done.

Continue the current external-review ingestion using ONLY the handoff file written by the previous evidence step. Do NOT discover the latest review artifact by timestamp. First read `codeInfoStatus/flow-state/current-plan.json` and use only the stored `plan_path` and `additional_repositories` as the active scope for this flow. Verify that the selected plan file exists in the current repository, that the story number in the current repository branch name matches the story number in the selected plan filename, and that every additional repository still matches the same story branch. If those do not match, stop and say the current-plan handoff is stale and must be regenerated; do not edit the plan. Then read `codeInfoStatus/reviews/<story-number>-current-review.json`, derived from the selected plan filename, and verify that its `story_id` matches the current plan, that its referenced `evidence_file` exists, that its `external_review_input_file` exists, and that every `repos` entry still matches the repository scope, branch name, resolved base branch, and HEAD commit. If any of those checks fail, stop and say the review handoff is stale and must be regenerated; do not edit the plan. If the handoff is valid, perform the actual review against the planned work, the per-repository branch diffs, and the external review input file. Treat the external review input as candidate comments, not as automatically valid findings. Repository review policy: `codeInfoStatus/**`, `flows/**`, and `codex_agents/**` are approved workflow-support paths. Do not raise findings solely because those paths changed without being named in the active plan. Review `codeInfoStatus/**` for artifact hygiene, durable-vs-transient artifact handling, and workflow-contract correctness. Review `flows/**` and `codex_agents/**` for workflow semantics, instruction safety, stale-handoff handling, commit/push behavior, plan-selection rules, and other agent-control correctness. This step MUST produce findings only and MUST NOT edit the plan yet. Write the findings to `codeInfoStatus/reviews/<review_pass_id>-findings.md` using findings-first ordering by severity, with file references and explicit classification of each finding as `must_fix`, `should_fix`, or `optional_simplification`. Also state for each finding whether it is a `plan_contract_issue` or a `generic_engineering_issue`. After the findings list, add a short section for rejected or non-adopted external review comments and explain why they were not accepted as findings. This findings file is a durable review artifact that MUST be committed later so a human can inspect it after the story completes. If no findings exist, state that explicitly and also record any residual risks or weak-proof areas. Update the same handoff file so `findings_file` points to the exact findings artifact, and include any useful counts or disposition hints. Do not commit in this step unless you were forced to make tracked changes to repair the review artifacts themselves.

Finish the current story review using ONLY the handoff file and findings file referenced by that handoff. Do NOT rediscover review artifacts by timestamp. First read `codeInfoStatus/flow-state/current-plan.json` and use only the stored `plan_path` and `additional_repositories` as the active scope for this flow. Verify that the selected plan file exists in the current repository, that the story number in the current repository branch name matches the story number in the selected plan filename, and that every additional repository still matches the same story branch. If those do not match, stop and say the current-plan handoff is stale and must be regenerated; do not edit the plan. Then read `codeInfoStatus/reviews/<story-number>-current-review.json`, derived from the selected plan filename, again and verify that its `story_id`, `review_pass_id`, `evidence_file`, `findings_file`, and `repos` entries still match the current repo state. If the handoff is stale or incomplete, stop and say the review must be rerun; do not edit the plan. If the handoff is valid, decide what the canonical plan must do with the findings. Repository review policy: treat `codeInfoStatus/**` as approved workflow artifacts, and treat `flows/**` plus `codex_agents/**` as approved workflow configuration. Do not reopen the story or record scope-creep findings solely because those paths changed without being named in the active plan. Only reopen if the review shows those changes introduced incorrect workflow behavior, broke repository contracts, or require explicit follow-up validation work. Rules: 1. If any `must_fix` or `should_fix` findings exist, reopen the canonical plan, add a `Code Review Findings` summary section to the END of the file, add explicit follow-up tasks using the same structure as previous tasks AFTER the newly added `Code Review Findings`, and add a fresh full re-test/final validation task after those review-fix tasks so the story must be revalidated against the acceptance criteria. Update numbering and cross-references if needed. 2. Every new review-fix task MUST name exactly one repository using `Repository Name`. 3. If only `optional_simplification` findings exist, reopen the canonical plan when the simplification is localized to files already changed by the story, low-risk, objectively testable, and improves a shared contract such as logging vocabulary, marker schema, or configuration consistency. Only defer it when the cleanup is speculative, broad, or not worth the churn; if it is deferred, record it in a short review note instead of reopening. 4. If there are no findings, append a `Post-Implementation Code Review` section to the end of the canonical plan detailing the branch-vs-base checks performed across all repositories in scope, the acceptance-evidence checks performed, the files inspected, and why the story remains complete. 5. The current pass `evidence_file` and `findings_file` are durable review artifacts and MUST be added to the commit alongside any plan changes so a human can inspect them later. The transient handoff file should not be treated as the durable artifact; once it has been consumed successfully, either remove it before committing or leave it untracked so later review passes do not rely on stale committed handoff state. 6. Commit the plan and the current pass review artifacts if you make tracked changes, but do not push. This is the only review step allowed to mutate the plan.
If possible, git push the story branch in the current repository and in every repository listed in `additional_repositories`.
